title: eremophila serrulata
intro: Eremophila serrulata, also known as Emu Bush, is a shrub native to Australia that belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Eremophila serrulata include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as wound healing and skin conditions. Eremophila serrulata can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal uses, and its essential oil has been used in aromatherapy for its calming effects.
